pattern: If the array can be cut into three equal-sum blocks, each block totals one third of the whole, so a single greedy scan that closes a block whenever the running sum reaches that target answers the question.
hints: Each of the three parts must sum to total divided by three, so if the total is not divisible by three the answer is immediately false.
Walk left to right accumulating a running sum; every time it equals the target, you have completed one part and can reset the accumulator.
Count how many parts you can close this way, but stop before consuming the whole array — you need to reach the target boundary two times with elements still left for the third part.

edge_inputs.description: almost the same but cannot partition
total is zero but cannot split into three nonempty zero parts
target reached early then trailing leftovers exceed it

stress.description: construct an array that genuinely splits into three equal blocks plus distractors
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, size)
    arr = [rng.randint(-50, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [arr]
stress.note: random; partitionable or not, both references must agree
